title: [BUG] Can't access data inside a table
author: okon3
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

I'm developing a web app using react and I need to read data inside and Excel Table. I can get the table using `worksheet.getTable()` but then I'm unable to **read** the data directly from the table and im forced to just cycle the rows of the entire worksheet.
From the documentation it seems that there should be a `rows` property in the table to access the data, but I keep getting errors as it's undefined in my case.

Here is the Excel i'm trying to read: [test.xlsx](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/files/11904782/test.xlsx)

Lib version: 4.3.0

## Steps To Reproduce

<!-- The exact steps required to reproduce the issue, ideally with a code example -->

```javascript
const workbook = new Workbook();
const xlsx = await workbook.xlsx.load(file);
const worksheet = xlsx.getWorksheet('test');
const table = worksheet.getTable('tbl_dictionary');

table.rows <---  undefined
table.table.rows <---  undefined

table.addRow(['test']) <--- throws error Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length')

```

## The expected behaviour:

I'm able to loop through table rows using `table.rows` or `table.table.rows`

comments.author: singhxamit21
lhousaine
comments.body:
@okon3  , yeah, I have already coming across to the same problem working on a professional project. @singhxamit21 using getColumn(1) works for me but it does not present any useful data about the table content as all properties are undefined as specified in the given screen

comments.author: hb-webdev
comments.body: I can't find a single (free) xlsx-reading library for Node.js that can read Cell contents BY TABLE NAME. This is insane to me.
